goal: theorem union_comm: for A, B being set holds A \/ B = B \/ A
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be set; thus A \/ B = B \/ A by XBOOLE_0:def 3; end;

goal: theorem inter_comm: for A, B being set holds A /\ B = B /\ A
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be set; thus A /\ B = B /\ A by XBOOLE_0:def 4; end;

goal: theorem union_assoc: for A, B, C being set holds A \/ (B \/ C) = (A \/ B) \/ C
tactic_proof: proof let A, B, C be set; thus thesis by XBOOLE_1:4; end;

goal: theorem inter_assoc: for A, B, C being set holds A /\ (B /\ C) = (A /\ B) /\ C
tactic_proof: proof let A, B, C be set; thus thesis by XBOOLE_1:16; end;

goal: theorem union_empty: for A being set holds A \/ {} = A
tactic_proof: proof let A be set; thus thesis by XBOOLE_1:1; end;

goal: theorem inter_univ: for A being set holds A /\ A = A
tactic_proof: proof let A be set; thus thesis by XBOOLE_1:17; end;

goal: theorem subset_refl: for A being set holds A c= A
tactic_proof: proof let A be set; let x be object; assume x in A; thus x in A; end;

goal: theorem subset_trans: for A, B, C being set st A c= B & B c= C holds A c= C
tactic_proof: proof let A, B, C be set; assume A1: A c= B; assume A2: B c= C; let x be object; assume x in A; then x in B by A1; hence x in C by A2; end;

goal: theorem subset_union_left: for A, B being set holds A c= A \/ B
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be set; let x be object; assume x in A; hence x in A \/ B by XBOOLE_0:def 3; end;

goal: theorem subset_inter_left: for A, B being set holds A /\ B c= A
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be set; let x be object; assume x in A /\ B; hence x in A by XBOOLE_0:def 4; end;

goal: theorem de_morgan_union: for A, B, X being set holds X \ (A \/ B) = (X \ A) /\ (X \ B)
tactic_proof: proof let A, B, X be set; thus thesis by XBOOLE_1:53; end;

goal: theorem de_morgan_inter: for A, B, X being set holds X \ (A /\ B) = (X \ A) \/ (X \ B)
tactic_proof: proof let A, B, X be set; thus thesis by XBOOLE_1:54; end;

goal: theorem empty_subset: for A being set holds {} c= A
tactic_proof: proof let A be set; thus {} c= A by XBOOLE_1:2; end;

goal: theorem subset_antisym: for A, B being set st A c= B & B c= A holds A = B
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be set; assume A c= B; assume B c= A; thus A = B by XBOOLE_0:def 10; end;

goal: theorem power_set_member: for A, B being set st A c= B holds A in bool B
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be set; assume A c= B; thus A in bool B by ZFMISC_1:def 1; end;

goal: theorem nat_add_comm: for m, n being Nat holds m + n = n + m
tactic_proof: proof let m, n be Nat; thus m + n = n + m by XCMPLX_0:def 3; end;

goal: theorem nat_add_assoc: for m, n, k being Nat holds (m + n) + k = m + (n + k)
tactic_proof: proof let m, n, k be Nat; thus (m + n) + k = m + (n + k) by XCMPLX_1:1; end;

goal: theorem nat_mul_comm: for m, n being Nat holds m * n = n * m
tactic_proof: proof let m, n be Nat; thus m * n = n * m by XCMPLX_0:def 4; end;

goal: theorem nat_mul_assoc: for m, n, k being Nat holds (m * n) * k = m * (n * k)
tactic_proof: proof let m, n, k be Nat; thus (m * n) * k = m * (n * k) by XCMPLX_1:4; end;

goal: theorem nat_distrib: for m, n, k being Nat holds m * (n + k) = m * n + m * k
tactic_proof: proof let m, n, k be Nat; thus m * (n + k) = m * n + m * k by XCMPLX_1:8; end;

goal: theorem nat_add_zero: for n being Nat holds n + 0 = n
tactic_proof: proof let n be Nat; thus n + 0 = n; end;

goal: theorem nat_mul_zero: for n being Nat holds n * 0 = 0
tactic_proof: proof let n be Nat; thus n * 0 = 0; end;

goal: theorem nat_mul_one: for n being Nat holds n * 1 = n
tactic_proof: proof let n be Nat; thus n * 1 = n; end;

goal: theorem nat_le_refl: for n being Nat holds n <= n
tactic_proof: proof let n be Nat; thus n <= n; end;

goal: theorem nat_le_antisym: for m, n being Nat st m <= n & n <= m holds m = n
tactic_proof: proof let m, n be Nat; assume A1: m <= n; assume A2: n <= m; thus m = n by A1, A2, XXREAL_0:1; end;

goal: theorem nat_le_trans: for m, n, k being Nat st m <= n & n <= k holds m <= k
tactic_proof: proof let m, n, k be Nat; assume m <= n; assume n <= k; hence m <= k by XXREAL_0:2; end;

goal: theorem nat_lt_succ: for n being Nat holds n < n + 1
tactic_proof: proof let n be Nat; thus n < n + 1 by NAT_1:13; end;

goal: theorem nat_induction_schema: for P being Nat-defined Function st P.0 is true & (for n being Nat st P.n is true holds P.(n+1) is true) holds for n being Nat holds P.n is true
tactic_proof: proof let P be Nat-defined Function; assume A1: P.0 is true; assume A2: for n being Nat st P.n is true holds P.(n+1) is true; defpred Q[Nat] means P.\$1 is true; A3: Q[0] by A1; A4: for n being Nat st Q[n] holds Q[n+1] by A2; thus for n being Nat holds Q[n] from NAT_1:sch 2(A3, A4); end;

goal: theorem nat_well_ordering: for X being non empty Subset of NAT ex m being Nat st m in X & for n being Nat st n in X holds m <= n
tactic_proof: proof let X be non empty Subset of NAT; thus thesis by NAT_1:def 1; end;

goal: theorem nat_div_mod: for a being Nat, b being non zero Nat holds a = b * (a div b) + (a mod b)
tactic_proof: proof let a be Nat, b be non zero Nat; thus a = b * (a div b) + (a mod b) by NAT_D:def 1, NAT_D:def 2; end;

goal: theorem real_add_comm: for x, y being Real holds x + y = y + x
tactic_proof: proof let x, y be Real; thus x + y = y + x by XCMPLX_0:def 3; end;

goal: theorem real_mul_comm: for x, y being Real holds x * y = y * x
tactic_proof: proof let x, y be Real; thus x * y = y * x by XCMPLX_0:def 4; end;

goal: theorem real_add_inv: for x being Real holds x + (-x) = 0
tactic_proof: proof let x be Real; thus x + (-x) = 0 by XCMPLX_0:def 6; end;

goal: theorem real_mul_inv: for x being non zero Real holds x * x" = 1
tactic_proof: proof let x be non zero Real; thus x * x" = 1 by XCMPLX_0:def 7; end;

goal: theorem real_abs_nonneg: for x being Real holds |.x.| >= 0
tactic_proof: proof let x be Real; thus |.x.| >= 0 by ABSVALUE:4; end;

goal: theorem real_abs_zero: for x being Real holds |.x.| = 0 iff x = 0
tactic_proof: proof let x be Real; thus |.x.| = 0 iff x = 0 by ABSVALUE:2; end;

goal: theorem real_triangle: for x, y being Real holds |.x + y.| <= |.x.| + |.y.|
tactic_proof: proof let x, y be Real; thus |.x + y.| <= |.x.| + |.y.| by ABSVALUE:13; end;

goal: theorem real_sq_nonneg: for x being Real holds x ^2 >= 0
tactic_proof: proof let x be Real; thus x ^2 >= 0 by XREAL_1:63; end;

goal: theorem real_archimedes: for x being positive Real ex n being Nat st x < n
tactic_proof: proof let x be positive Real; thus ex n being Nat st x < n by INT_1:37; end;

goal: theorem real_complete: for A being non empty bounded_above Subset of REAL ex s being Real st s is_least_upper_bound_of A
tactic_proof: proof let A be non empty bounded_above Subset of REAL; thus thesis by XXREAL_2:28; end;

goal: theorem func_comp_assoc: for f, g, h being Function st rng h c= dom g & rng g c= dom f holds (f * g) * h = f * (g * h)
tactic_proof: proof let f, g, h be Function; assume A1: rng h c= dom g; assume A2: rng g c= dom f; thus (f * g) * h = f * (g * h) by A1, A2, RELAT_1:36; end;

goal: theorem func_id_comp: for f being Function holds f * (id dom f) = f
tactic_proof: proof let f be Function; thus f * (id dom f) = f by FUNCT_1:22; end;

goal: theorem func_injective_comp: for f, g being Function st f is one-to-one & g is one-to-one & rng g c= dom f holds f * g is one-to-one
tactic_proof: proof let f, g be Function; assume A1: f is one-to-one; assume A2: g is one-to-one; assume A3: rng g c= dom f; thus f * g is one-to-one by A1, A2, A3, FUNCT_1:27; end;

goal: theorem func_surjective_comp: for f, g being Function st f is onto & g is onto holds f * g is onto
tactic_proof: proof let f, g be Function; assume f is onto; assume g is onto; thus f * g is onto by FUNCT_1:28; end;

goal: theorem func_inverse: for f being one-to-one Function holds f " * f = id dom f
tactic_proof: proof let f be one-to-one Function; thus f " * f = id dom f by FUNCT_1:44; end;

goal: theorem func_image_union: for f being Function, A, B being set holds f.:( A \/ B) = f.:A \/ f.:B
tactic_proof: proof let f be Function, A, B be set; thus f.:(A \/ B) = f.:A \/ f.:B by RELAT_1:120; end;

goal: theorem func_image_inter_inj: for f being one-to-one Function, A, B being set holds f.:(A /\ B) = f.:A /\ f.:B
tactic_proof: proof let f be one-to-one Function, A, B be set; thus f.:(A /\ B) = f.:A /\ f.:B by FUNCT_1:72; end;

goal: theorem func_preimage_union: for f being Function, A, B being set holds f"(A \/ B) = f"A \/ f"B
tactic_proof: proof let f be Function, A, B be set; thus f"(A \/ B) = f"A \/ f"B by RELAT_1:171; end;

goal: theorem open_empty: for T being TopSpace holds {} is open Subset of T
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace; thus {} is open Subset of T by PRE_TOPC:def 2; end;

goal: theorem open_carrier: for T being TopSpace holds [#]T is open Subset of T
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace; thus [#]T is open Subset of T by PRE_TOPC:def 2; end;

goal: theorem open_union: for T being TopSpace, F being Subset-Family of T st for A being Subset of T st A in F holds A is open holds union F is open Subset of T
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace; let F be Subset-Family of T; assume for A being Subset of T st A in F holds A is open; thus union F is open Subset of T by PRE_TOPC:def 2; end;

goal: theorem closed_empty: for T being TopSpace holds {} is closed Subset of T
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace; thus {} is closed Subset of T by PRE_TOPC:3; end;

goal: theorem closed_carrier: for T being TopSpace holds [#]T is closed Subset of T
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace; thus [#]T is closed Subset of T by PRE_TOPC:3; end;

goal: theorem closure_subset: for T being TopSpace, A being Subset of T holds A c= Cl A
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace, A be Subset of T; thus A c= Cl A by PRE_TOPC:18; end;

goal: theorem closure_closed: for T being TopSpace, A being Subset of T holds Cl A is closed
tactic_proof: proof let T be TopSpace, A be Subset of T; thus Cl A is closed by PRE_TOPC:def 6; end;

goal: theorem continuous_preimage_open: for f being continuous Function of T1, T2, V being open Subset of T2 holds f"V is open Subset of T1
tactic_proof: proof let f be continuous Function of T1, T2; let V be open Subset of T2; thus f"V is open Subset of T1 by TOPS_2:43; end;

goal: theorem compact_closed_subset: for T being compact TopSpace, A being closed Subset of T holds A is compact
tactic_proof: proof let T be compact TopSpace; let A be closed Subset of T; thus A is compact by COMPTS_1:8; end;

goal: theorem connected_image: for f being continuous Function of T1, T2 st T1 is connected holds f.:[#]T1 is connected Subset of T2
tactic_proof: proof let f be continuous Function of T1, T2; assume T1 is connected; thus f.:[#]T1 is connected Subset of T2 by CONNSP_1:15; end;

goal: theorem group_id_unique: for G being Group holds for e1, e2 being Element of G st (for a being Element of G holds e1 * a = a & a * e1 = a) & (for a being Element of G holds e2 * a = a & a * e2 = a) holds e1 = e2
tactic_proof: proof let G be Group; let e1, e2 be Element of G; assume A1: for a being Element of G holds e1 * a = a & a * e1 = a; assume A2: for a being Element of G holds e2 * a = a & a * e2 = a; thus e1 = e1 * e2 by A2 .= e2 by A1; end;

goal: theorem group_inv_unique: for G being Group, a, b1, b2 being Element of G st a * b1 = 1_G & a * b2 = 1_G holds b1 = b2
tactic_proof: proof let G be Group; let a, b1, b2 be Element of G; assume A1: a * b1 = 1_G; assume A2: a * b2 = 1_G; thus b1 = (a\") * a * b1 by GROUP_1:def 5 .= (a\") * (a * b1) by GROUP_1:def 3 .= (a\") * 1_G by A1 .= a\" by GROUP_1:def 4 .= (a\") * (a * b2) by A2 .= (a\") * a * b2 by GROUP_1:def 3 .= 1_G * b2 by GROUP_1:def 5 .= b2 by GROUP_1:def 4; end;

goal: theorem group_inv_inv: for G being Group, a being Element of G holds (a\")\" = a
tactic_proof: proof let G be Group; let a be Element of G; thus (a\")\" = a by GROUP_1:8; end;

goal: theorem group_inv_prod: for G being Group, a, b being Element of G holds (a * b)\" = b\" * a\"
tactic_proof: proof let G be Group; let a, b be Element of G; thus (a * b)\" = b\" * a\" by GROUP_1:17; end;

goal: theorem subgroup_criterion: for G being Group, H being non empty Subset of G st (for a, b being Element of G st a in H & b in H holds a * b\" in H) holds H is Subgroup of G
tactic_proof: proof let G be Group; let H be non empty Subset of G; assume for a, b being Element of G st a in H & b in H holds a * b\" in H; thus H is Subgroup of G by GROUP_2:52; end;

goal: theorem ring_add_comm: for R being Ring, a, b being Element of R holds a + b = b + a
tactic_proof: proof let R be Ring; let a, b be Element of R; thus a + b = b + a by RLVECT_1:2; end;

goal: theorem ring_mul_assoc: for R being Ring, a, b, c being Element of R holds (a * b) * c = a * (b * c)
tactic_proof: proof let R be Ring; let a, b, c be Element of R; thus (a * b) * c = a * (b * c) by GROUP_1:def 3; end;

goal: theorem ring_distrib_left: for R being Ring, a, b, c being Element of R holds a * (b + c) = a * b + a * c
tactic_proof: proof let R be Ring; let a, b, c be Element of R; thus a * (b + c) = a * b + a * c by VECTSP_1:def 7; end;

goal: theorem seq_convergent: for seq being Real_Sequence st seq is convergent holds for e being Real st e > 0 ex N being Nat st for n being Nat st n >= N holds |.seq.n - lim seq.| < e
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume A1: seq is convergent; let e be Real; assume A2: e > 0; thus ex N being Nat st for n being Nat st n >= N holds |.seq.n - lim seq.| < e by A1, A2, SEQ_2:def 7; end;

goal: theorem seq_monotone_incr: for seq being Real_Sequence st seq is increasing & seq is bounded_above holds seq is convergent
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume A1: seq is increasing; assume A2: seq is bounded_above; thus seq is convergent by A1, A2, SEQ_4:37; end;

goal: theorem seq_bounded_convergent: for seq being Real_Sequence st seq is convergent holds seq is bounded
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume seq is convergent; hence seq is bounded by SEQ_2:13; end;

goal: theorem seq_bolzano_weierstrass: for seq being Real_Sequence st seq is bounded ex seq1 being subsequence of seq st seq1 is convergent
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume A1: seq is bounded; consider seq1 being subsequence of seq such that A2: seq1 is monotone by A1, SEQ_4:39; take seq1; seq1 is bounded by A1, SEQ_4:27; hence seq1 is convergent by A2, SEQ_4:37, SEQ_4:38; end;

goal: theorem seq_cauchy_criterion: for seq being Real_Sequence holds seq is convergent iff seq is Cauchy
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; thus seq is convergent iff seq is Cauchy by SEQ_4:25; end;

goal: theorem seq_limit_add: for seq1, seq2 being Real_Sequence st seq1 is convergent & seq2 is convergent holds lim (seq1 + seq2) = lim seq1 + lim seq2
tactic_proof: proof let seq1, seq2 be Real_Sequence; assume A1: seq1 is convergent; assume A2: seq2 is convergent; thus lim (seq1 + seq2) = lim seq1 + lim seq2 by A1, A2, SEQ_2:6; end;

goal: theorem seq_limit_mul: for seq1, seq2 being Real_Sequence st seq1 is convergent & seq2 is convergent holds lim (seq1 (#) seq2) = (lim seq1) * (lim seq2)
tactic_proof: proof let seq1, seq2 be Real_Sequence; assume A1: seq1 is convergent; assume A2: seq2 is convergent; thus lim (seq1 (#) seq2) = (lim seq1) * (lim seq2) by A1, A2, SEQ_2:8; end;

goal: theorem seq_subsequence_limit: for seq being Real_Sequence, seq1 being subsequence of seq st seq is convergent holds seq1 is convergent & lim seq1 = lim seq
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; let seq1 be subsequence of seq; assume A1: seq is convergent; thus seq1 is convergent by A1, SEQ_4:17; hence lim seq1 = lim seq by A1, SEQ_4:17; end;

goal: theorem seq_series_convergent: for seq being Real_Sequence st seq is summable holds Partial_Sums seq is convergent
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume seq is summable; hence Partial_Sums seq is convergent by SERIES_1:def 2; end;

goal: theorem seq_ratio_test: for seq being Real_Sequence st (ex N being Nat st for n being Nat st n >= N holds |.seq.(n+1).| / |.seq.n.| < 1) holds seq is summable
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume ex N being Nat st for n being Nat st n >= N holds |.seq.(n+1).| / |.seq.n.| < 1; hence seq is summable by SERIES_1:24; end;

goal: theorem seq_comparison_test: for seq1, seq2 being Real_Sequence st (for n being Nat holds |.seq1.n.| <= seq2.n) & seq2 is summable holds seq1 is summable
tactic_proof: proof let seq1, seq2 be Real_Sequence; assume A1: for n being Nat holds |.seq1.n.| <= seq2.n; assume A2: seq2 is summable; thus seq1 is summable by A1, A2, SERIES_1:20; end;

goal: theorem seq_harmonic_divergent: for seq being Real_Sequence st for n being Nat holds seq.n = 1 / (n + 1) holds not seq is summable
tactic_proof: proof let seq be Real_Sequence; assume A1: for n being Nat holds seq.n = 1 / (n + 1); set P = Partial_Sums seq; assume A2: seq is summable; then P is convergent by SERIES_1:def 2; consider M being Real such that A3: for n being Nat holds P.n <= M by RSSPACE:7; per cases; suppose M > 0; consider N being Nat such that A4: P.N > M by A1, SEQ_4:6; thus contradiction by A3, A4; end; suppose M <= 0; P.0 = seq.0 by SERIES_1:def 1 .= 1 / (0 + 1) by A1 .= 1; hence contradiction by A3; end; end;

goal: theorem lattice_join_comm: for L being Lattice, a, b being Element of L holds a "\\/" b = b "\\/" a
tactic_proof: proof let L be Lattice; let a, b be Element of L; thus a "\\/" b = b "\\/" a by LATTICES:def 1; end;

goal: theorem lattice_meet_comm: for L being Lattice, a, b being Element of L holds a "/\" b = b "/\" a
tactic_proof: proof let L be Lattice; let a, b be Element of L; thus a "/\" b = b "/\" a by LATTICES:def 2; end;

goal: theorem lattice_join_assoc: for L being Lattice, a, b, c being Element of L holds a "\\/" (b "\\/" c) = (a "\\/" b) "\\/" c
tactic_proof: proof let L be Lattice; let a, b, c be Element of L; thus a "\\/" (b "\\/" c) = (a "\\/" b) "\\/" c by LATTICES:def 5; end;

goal: theorem lattice_meet_assoc: for L being Lattice, a, b, c being Element of L holds a "/\" (b "/\" c) = (a "/\" b) "/\" c
tactic_proof: proof let L be Lattice; let a, b, c be Element of L; thus a "/\" (b "/\" c) = (a "/\" b) "/\" c by LATTICES:def 7; end;

goal: theorem lattice_absorption_join: for L being Lattice, a, b being Element of L holds a "\\/" (a "/\" b) = a
tactic_proof: proof let L be Lattice; let a, b be Element of L; thus a "\\/" (a "/\" b) = a by LATTICES:def 8; end;

goal: theorem lattice_absorption_meet: for L being Lattice, a, b being Element of L holds a "/\" (a "\\/" b) = a
tactic_proof: proof let L be Lattice; let a, b be Element of L; thus a "/\" (a "\\/" b) = a by LATTICES:def 9; end;

goal: theorem lattice_distributive: for L being distributive Lattice, a, b, c being Element of L holds a "/\" (b "\\/" c) = (a "/\" b) "\\/" (a "/\" c)
tactic_proof: proof let L be distributive Lattice; let a, b, c be Element of L; thus a "/\" (b "\\/" c) = (a "/\" b) "\\/" (a "/\" c) by LATTICES:def 11; end;

goal: theorem lattice_complemented: for L being complemented bounded Lattice, a being Element of L ex b being Element of L st a "\\/" b = Top L & a "/\" b = Bottom L
tactic_proof: proof let L be complemented bounded Lattice; let a be Element of L; thus ex b being Element of L st a "\\/" b = Top L & a "/\" b = Bottom L by LATTICES:def 19; end;

goal: theorem lattice_bounded: for L being bounded Lattice, a being Element of L holds a "\\/" (Bottom L) = a & a "/\" (Top L) = a
tactic_proof: proof let L be bounded Lattice; let a be Element of L; thus a "\\/" (Bottom L) = a by LATTICES:39; thus a "/\" (Top L) = a by LATTICES:41; end;

goal: theorem lattice_modular: for L being modular Lattice, a, b, c being Element of L st a [= c holds a "\\/" (b "/\" c) = (a "\\/" b) "/\" c
tactic_proof: proof let L be modular Lattice; let a, b, c be Element of L; assume a [= c; hence a "\\/" (b "/\" c) = (a "\\/" b) "/\" c by LATTICES:def 12; end;

goal: theorem metric_dist_nonneg: for M being MetrSpace, p, q being Point of M holds dist(p, q) >= 0
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let p, q be Point of M; thus dist(p, q) >= 0 by METRIC_1:5; end;

goal: theorem metric_dist_sym: for M being MetrSpace, p, q being Point of M holds dist(p, q) = dist(q, p)
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let p, q be Point of M; thus dist(p, q) = dist(q, p) by METRIC_1:def 1; end;

goal: theorem metric_triangle_ineq: for M being MetrSpace, p, q, r being Point of M holds dist(p, r) <= dist(p, q) + dist(q, r)
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let p, q, r be Point of M; thus dist(p, r) <= dist(p, q) + dist(q, r) by METRIC_1:4; end;

goal: theorem metric_dist_zero: for M being MetrSpace, p, q being Point of M holds dist(p, q) = 0 iff p = q
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let p, q be Point of M; thus dist(p, q) = 0 iff p = q by METRIC_1:2, METRIC_1:1; end;

goal: theorem metric_open_ball_open: for M being MetrSpace, p being Point of M, r being positive Real holds Ball(p, r) is open Subset of TopSpaceMetr M
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let p be Point of M; let r be positive Real; thus Ball(p, r) is open Subset of TopSpaceMetr M by TBSP_1:16; end;

goal: theorem metric_cauchy_convergent: for M being MetrSpace, seq being sequence of M st M is complete & seq is Cauchy holds seq is convergent
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let seq be sequence of M; assume A1: M is complete; assume A2: seq is Cauchy; thus seq is convergent by A1, A2, TBSP_1:def 6; end;

goal: theorem metric_complete_closed: for M being MetrSpace, A being Subset of M st M is complete & A is closed holds A is complete
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let A be Subset of M; assume A1: M is complete; assume A2: A is closed; let seq be sequence of M such that A3: rng seq c= A and A4: seq is Cauchy; seq is convergent by A1, A4, TBSP_1:def 6; consider p being Point of M such that A5: seq is_convergent_in_metrspace_to p by TBSP_1:def 4; p in A by A2, A3, A5, TBSP_1:8; hence thesis; end;

goal: theorem metric_compact_sequentially: for M being MetrSpace, A being Subset of M st A is compact holds for seq being sequence of M st rng seq c= A ex seq1 being subsequence of seq st seq1 is convergent
tactic_proof: proof let M be MetrSpace; let A be Subset of M; assume A1: A is compact; let seq be sequence of M; assume rng seq c= A; thus ex seq1 being subsequence of seq st seq1 is convergent by A1, TBSP_1:def 9; end;

goal: theorem metric_lipschitz_continuous: for M1, M2 being MetrSpace, f being Function of M1, M2, L being positive Real st for p, q being Point of M1 holds dist(f.p, f.q) <= L * dist(p, q) holds f is continuous
tactic_proof: proof let M1, M2 be MetrSpace; let f be Function of M1, M2; let L be positive Real; assume A1: for p, q being Point of M1 holds dist(f.p, f.q) <= L * dist(p, q); let p be Point of M1; let e be Real; assume A2: e > 0; set d = e / L; take d; thus d > 0 by A2; let q be Point of M1; assume dist(p, q) < d; dist(f.p, f.q) <= L * dist(p, q) by A1; hence dist(f.p, f.q) < e by XREAL_1:68; end;

goal: theorem metric_equiv_open_sets: for M1, M2 being MetrSpace st the TopStruct of TopSpaceMetr M1 = the TopStruct of TopSpaceMetr M2 holds for A being Subset of M1 holds A is open Subset of TopSpaceMetr M1 iff A is open Subset of TopSpaceMetr M2
tactic_proof: proof let M1, M2 be MetrSpace; assume A1: the TopStruct of TopSpaceMetr M1 = the TopStruct of TopSpaceMetr M2; let A be Subset of M1; thus A is open Subset of TopSpaceMetr M1 iff A is open Subset of TopSpaceMetr M2 by A1, PRE_TOPC:def 2; end;

goal: theorem finseq_empty: for D being non empty set holds <*>D is FinSequence of D & len (<*>D) = 0
tactic_proof: proof let D be non empty set; thus <*>D is FinSequence of D; thus len (<*>D) = 0 by FINSEQ_1:def 3; end;

goal: theorem finseq_concat_len: for f, g being FinSequence holds len (f ^ g) = len f + len g
tactic_proof: proof let f, g be FinSequence; thus len (f ^ g) = len f + len g by FINSEQ_1:22; end;

goal: theorem finseq_concat_assoc: for f, g, h being FinSequence holds (f ^ g) ^ h = f ^ (g ^ h)
tactic_proof: proof let f, g, h be FinSequence; thus (f ^ g) ^ h = f ^ (g ^ h) by FINSEQ_1:32; end;

goal: theorem finseq_rev_rev: for f being FinSequence holds Rev (Rev f) = f
tactic_proof: proof let f be FinSequence; thus Rev (Rev f) = f by FINSEQ_1:48; end;

goal: theorem finseq_rev_len: for f being FinSequence holds len (Rev f) = len f
tactic_proof: proof let f be FinSequence; thus len (Rev f) = len f by FINSEQ_1:46; end;

goal: theorem finseq_singleton: for x being object holds <*x*> is FinSequence & len <*x*> = 1
tactic_proof: proof let x be object; thus <*x*> is FinSequence; thus len <*x*> = 1 by FINSEQ_1:39; end;

goal: theorem finseq_dom_range: for f being FinSequence holds dom f = Seg len f
tactic_proof: proof let f be FinSequence; thus dom f = Seg len f by FINSEQ_1:def 3; end;

goal: theorem finseq_rng_subset: for D being set, f being FinSequence of D holds rng f c= D
tactic_proof: proof let D be set; let f be FinSequence of D; thus rng f c= D by FINSEQ_1:def 4; end;

goal: theorem finseq_perm_len: for f, g being FinSequence st g is Permutation of f holds len g = len f
tactic_proof: proof let f, g be FinSequence; assume g is Permutation of f; hence len g = len f by FINSEQ_2:32; end;

goal: theorem finseq_concat_empty: for f being FinSequence holds f ^ (<*> the carrier of REAL) = f
tactic_proof: proof let f be FinSequence; thus f ^ (<*> the carrier of REAL) = f by FINSEQ_1:34; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_lt_irrefl: for A being Ordinal holds not A in A
tactic_proof: proof let A be Ordinal; thus not A in A by ORDINAL1:5; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_lt_trans: for A, B, C being Ordinal st A in B & B in C holds A in C
tactic_proof: proof let A, B, C be Ordinal; assume A1: A in B; assume A2: B in C; thus A in C by A1, A2, ORDINAL1:10; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_trichotomy: for A, B being Ordinal holds A in B or A = B or B in A
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be Ordinal; thus A in B or A = B or B in A by ORDINAL1:16; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_succ_gt: for A being Ordinal holds A in succ A
tactic_proof: proof let A be Ordinal; thus A in succ A by ORDINAL1:6; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_succ_ordinal: for A being Ordinal holds succ A is Ordinal
tactic_proof: proof let A be Ordinal; thus succ A is Ordinal by ORDINAL1:def 4; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_limit_not_succ: for A being Ordinal st A is limit_ordinal & A <> {} holds not ex B being Ordinal st A = succ B
tactic_proof: proof let A be Ordinal; assume A1: A is limit_ordinal; assume A2: A <> {}; assume ex B being Ordinal st A = succ B; then consider B being Ordinal such that A3: A = succ B; thus contradiction by A1, A3, ORDINAL1:def 5; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_transfinite_ind: for P being Ordinal-defined Function st (for A being Ordinal st (for B being Ordinal st B in A holds P.B is true) holds P.A is true) holds for A being Ordinal holds P.A is true
tactic_proof: proof let P be Ordinal-defined Function; assume A1: for A being Ordinal st (for B being Ordinal st B in A holds P.B is true) holds P.A is true; defpred Q[Ordinal] means P.\$1 is true; A2: for A being Ordinal st (for B being Ordinal st B in A holds Q[B]) holds Q[A] by A1; thus for A being Ordinal holds Q[A] from ORDINAL1:sch 3(A2); end;

goal: theorem ordinal_add_zero: for A being Ordinal holds A + {} = A
tactic_proof: proof let A be Ordinal; thus A + {} = A by ORDINAL2:27; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_add_succ: for A, B being Ordinal holds A + succ B = succ (A + B)
tactic_proof: proof let A, B be Ordinal; thus A + succ B = succ (A + B) by ORDINAL2:28; end;

goal: theorem ordinal_mul_zero: for A being Ordinal holds A *^ {} = {}
tactic_proof: proof let A be Ordinal; thus A *^ {} = {} by ORDINAL2:35; end;

goal: theorem measure_sigma_algebra_empty: for S being SigmaField of X holds {} in S
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; thus {} in S by PROB_1:4; end;

goal: theorem measure_sigma_algebra_compl: for S being SigmaField of X, A being set st A in S holds X \ A in S
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let A be set; assume A in S; hence X \ A in S by PROB_1:def 1; end;

goal: theorem measure_sigma_algebra_union: for S being SigmaField of X, A being SetSequence of X st (for n being Nat holds A.n in S) holds union rng A in S
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let A be SetSequence of X; assume for n being Nat holds A.n in S; hence union rng A in S by PROB_1:def 1; end;

goal: theorem measure_nonneg: for S being SigmaField of X, M being Measure of S, A being set st A in S holds M.A >= 0
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let M be Measure of S; let A be set; assume A in S; thus M.A >= 0 by SUPINF_2:42, MEASURE1:def 2; end;

goal: theorem measure_empty_zero: for S being SigmaField of X, M being Measure of S holds M.{} = 0
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let M be Measure of S; thus M.{} = 0 by MEASURE1:def 2; end;

goal: theorem measure_countable_additivity: for S being SigmaField of X, M being Measure of S, A being SetSequence of X st A is mutually-disjoint & (for n being Nat holds A.n in S) holds M.(union rng A) = SUM(M * A)
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let M be Measure of S; let A be SetSequence of X; assume A1: A is mutually-disjoint; assume A2: for n being Nat holds A.n in S; thus M.(union rng A) = SUM(M * A) by A1, A2, MEASURE1:def 2; end;

goal: theorem measure_monotone: for S being SigmaField of X, M being Measure of S, A, B being set st A in S & B in S & A c= B holds M.A <= M.B
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let M be Measure of S; let A, B be set; assume A1: A in S; assume A2: B in S; assume A3: A c= B; reconsider C = B \ A as Element of S by A1, A2, PROB_1:def 1; M.B = M.A + M.C by A1, A3, MEASURE1:22; hence M.A <= M.B by SUPINF_2:42, MEASURE1:def 2; end;

goal: theorem measure_subadditivity: for S being SigmaField of X, M being Measure of S, A being SetSequence of X st (for n being Nat holds A.n in S) holds M.(union rng A) <= SUM(M * A)
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let M be Measure of S; let A be SetSequence of X; assume for n being Nat holds A.n in S; hence M.(union rng A) <= SUM(M * A) by MEASURE1:31; end;

goal: theorem measurable_function_preimage: for S being SigmaField of X, f being Function of X, REAL, a being Real st f is S-measurable holds f"(].a, +infty.[) in S
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let f be Function of X, REAL; let a be Real; assume f is S-measurable; hence f"(].a, +infty.[) in S by MEASURE1:def 7; end;

goal: theorem measure_continuity_below: for S being SigmaField of X, M being Measure of S, A being SetSequence of X st (for n being Nat holds A.n in S) & A is non-descending holds M.(union rng A) = lim (M * A)
tactic_proof: proof let S be SigmaField of X; let M be Measure of S; let A be SetSequence of X; assume A1: for n being Nat holds A.n in S; assume A2: A is non-descending; thus M.(union rng A) = lim (M * A) by A1, A2, MEASURE1:32; end;
